title: Tata Steel Zoological Park
tagline: Tata Steel Zoological Park — wildlife reserve of Jharkhand

overview.short: Tata Steel Zoological Park (TSZP) is situated in a corner of Jubilee Park, Jamshedpur.
overview.description: Tata Steel Zoological Park (TSZP) is situated in a corner of Jubilee Park, Jamshedpur. TSZP is a recognized 'Medium' category Zoo by the Central Zoo Authority (CZA). The CZA evaluates Zoos on periodic basis and stipulates conditions for their improvement. TSZP has been evaluated by CZA at periodic intervals and major recommendations to meet the prescribed standards are creation of a veterinary hospital with postmortem room and incinerator, shifting of animal enclosures, separate entry and exit gates for visiting public, strengthening perimeter wall etc.

topPlaces.name: Jubilee Park, Jamshedpur
topPlaces.description: Jubilee Park is an urban park located in Jamshedpur, Jharkhand, India. It is a popular destination for all those who wish to have an outdoor picnic, enjoy some outdoor activities and games or even just to have a relaxing day out with friends and family.

topPlaces.name: JRD Tata Sports Complex
topPlaces.description: The JRD Tata Sports Complex Stadium, also known as The Furnace, is a 40,000-capacity (often limited to 24,424) stadium in Jamshedpur, India. It is used mostly for association football matches and athletics competitions.

topPlaces.name: Keenan Stadium
topPlaces.description: Keenan Stadium, is a multi-purpose stadium and an International Cricket Stadium in Jamshedpur, India. It is currently used mostly for cricket and football matches.

topPlaces.name: Sakchi
topPlaces.description: Sakchi is one of the oldest neighbourhoods in the city of Jamshedpur, Jharkhand, India. Situated in the heart of the city, it is surrounded by Bistupur to the west, Swarnarekha River to the north, Kalimati to the south and Bhalubasa to the east.

topPlaces.name: Al Kabir Polytechnic
topPlaces.description: Al-Kabir Polytechnic, also known as Al Kabir Institute of Management and Technology, is a private institute in Jamshedpur, Jharkhand, India. Established in 1990 by Abdul Hakim, it is one of the oldest private polytechnic institutes in eastern India.

topPlaces.name: Mango (Jamshedpur)
topPlaces.description: Mango is a suburb in Jamshedpur, Jharkhand. The city is situated across the Subarnarkeha River and is connected to Jamshedpur via three bridges laid side by side.

topPlaces.name: Kopali
topPlaces.description: Kopali (also spelled as Kapali) is a census town in the Chandil CD block in the Chandil subdivision of the Seraikela Kharsawan district in the Indian state of Jharkhand.

topPlaces.name: Indian Powerlifting Federation
topPlaces.description: The Indian Powerlifting Federation is a national governing body for the sport of powerlifting as recognized by the Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports, Government of India as well as The Indian Olympic Association. It currently has over 10,000 members throughout the country.

items.note: Jubilee Park is an urban park located in Jamshedpur, Jharkhand, India. It is a popular destination for all those who wish to have an outdoor picnic, enjoy some outdoor activities and games or even just to have a relaxing day out with friends and family.

The JRD Tata Sports Complex Stadium, also known as The Furnace, is a 40,000-capacity (often limited to 24,424) stadium in Jamshedpur, India. It is used mostly for association football matches and athletics competitions.
Keenan Stadium, is a multi-purpose stadium and an International Cricket Stadium in Jamshedpur, India. It is currently used mostly for cricket and football matches.

Sakchi is one of the oldest neighbourhoods in the city of Jamshedpur, Jharkhand, India. Situated in the heart of the city, it is surrounded by Bistupur to the west, Swarnarekha River to the north, Kalimati to the south and Bhalubasa to the east.

Al-Kabir Polytechnic, also known as Al Kabir Institute of Management and Technology, is a private institute in Jamshedpur, Jharkhand, India. Established in 1990 by Abdul Hakim, it is one of the oldest private polytechnic institutes in eastern India.
Mango is a suburb in Jamshedpur, Jharkhand. The city is situated across the Subarnarkeha River and is connected to Jamshedpur via three bridges laid side by side.
Kopali (also spelled as Kapali) is a census town in the Chandil CD block in the Chandil subdivision of the Seraikela Kharsawan district in the Indian state of Jharkhand.

The Indian Powerlifting Federation is a national governing body for the sport of powerlifting as recognized by the Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports, Government of India as well as The Indian Olympic Association. It currently has over 10,000 members throughout the country.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Tata Steel Zoological Park?
faq.a: The best time to visit Tata Steel Zoological Park is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Tata Steel Zoological Park?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Birsa Munda Airport, Ranchi (105 km) and the nearest railway station is Jamshedpur (Tatanagar) (3 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Tata Steel Zoological Park?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Tata Steel Zoological Park at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Tata Steel Zoological Park?
faq.a: Stays in Tata Steel Zoological Park start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Tata Steel Zoological Park?
faq.a: The top attractions are Jubilee Park, Jamshedpur, JRD Tata Sports Complex, Keenan Stadium, Sakchi, Al Kabir Polytechnic.
